text:
Setting clear project goals is essential for success. A useful method is to set SMART goals, which means they are Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-bound. For example, instead of saying "improve the website," a SMART goal would be to "increase website traffic by 15% within three months."

To track progress, break the project into milestones. These are key points that show you are moving forward. You also need to define deliverables, which are the tangible results you will produce, like a report or a new feature.

Finally, stakeholder alignment is crucial. This means making sure everyone involved agrees on the goals and expectations. Regular communication helps avoid confusion and keeps the project on track.
